tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal expected movement over the next ~16 months, with a median of $66,865 (essentially flat from current $66,799 spot). The 10th-90th percentile range of $65,153-$67,897 reflects modest price dispersion, suggesting moderate consensus around near-term consolidation with limited downside conviction below $65k.

One day later, the median forecast remains unchanged at $66,811, indicating no material shift in market expectations. However, the widened 90th percentile to $69,021 signals slightly greater upside optionality, while the compressed 10th percentile at $64,520 suggests tightening downside risk assessment.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets with modest volume ($83.7k) expect WTI to rally 13.9% to a median of $127/bbl by April 2026, with a wide range reflecting uncertainty—25th percentile at $103 and 90th percentile at $158. The interquartile spread suggests meaningful probability mass both below and above current levels, though the median implies a modest upside bias.

With substantially higher conviction ($987.7k volume), markets predict a significant 26.2% decline to $82/bbl median by June 2026, well below the April forecast. However, the 25th-75th percentile range ($71-$148) reveals deep disagreement, with the 90th percentile at $197 indicating tail risks to much higher prices, suggesting markets are pricing both mean-reversion downside and potential supply shock scenarios.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect gold to decline 2.6% to $4,553 by mid-2026, with a wide 80% confidence interval spanning $3,976 to $5,394. The distribution suggests meaningful downside risk to $3,643 (10th percentile) but also tail upside to $5,961 (90th percentile), reflecting significant uncertainty around medium-term gold dynamics.
